theme_notes: Touchstones: Keir Starmer's surprise Monday resignation as UK PM, the US-Iran nuclear deal 60-day roadmap (VP Vance in Zurich, gas finally dipping below $4), the 2026 FIFA World Cup (Messi breaks all-time scoring record, Canada 6-0 Qatar), and House of the Dragon Season 3 premiering June 21 with a Battle of Gullet death.
settled: Zero. Like the US-Iran deal — technically holding, universally skeptical.
Balanced. Keir Starmer resigned today. One crisis resolved at a time.
Cleared. House of the Dragon just premiered. All things stable are temporary.
youOwe: Less than a World Cup stadium Coke. You are the Qatar of this household.
Less than what Keir Starmer's resignation letter was worth. Roughly the same energy.
Amazon Prime Day is Tuesday. Close the app. Pay this first.
Canada beat Qatar 6-0. You have Qatar's exact energy about paying this.
This debt now has its own House of the Dragon arc: slow, chaotic, someone's getting burned.
The US and Iran agreed on a 60-day timeline. You have no timeline.
Keir Starmer's entire cabinet survived longer than your intention to pay this.
That's a roundtrip to a World Cup group stage game. You went nowhere near paying.
Gas finally dropped below $4. You're still the most expensive thing going.
Messi broke the all-time World Cup scoring record today. You broke something too.
You've got House of the Dragon energy: everything on fire, no one taking responsibility.
The UK lost a Prime Minister today. You might lose a co-signer. Big Monday.
Vance flew to Switzerland to close a nuclear deal. Nobody's flying in to close yours.
World Cup hospitality package territory. You're watching from someone else's couch.
A major House of the Dragon character died last night. Your credit score is following.
Iran agreed to nuclear disarmament faster than you'll agree to a payment plan.
Canada crushed Qatar 6-0. That's also how this debt is going against your dignity.
Keir Starmer's political legacy ended today. Your financial credibility ended first.
theyOwe: They owe you next to nothing. You're auditing it like a World Cup VAR replay.
Barely a debt. Like House of the Dragon premiere deaths — noted, surprisingly expected.
Less than Prime Day shipping. Still on the ledger. As it should be.
They owe you. Keir Starmer owes Britain an explanation. At least yours is quantifiable.
World Cup group-stage parking money. You're still owed it.
The US-Iran roadmap has more structure than their repayment plan.
You funded this partnership like America funded the World Cup: silently and completely.
Gas dropped below $4 for the first time in months. This still costs more.
House of the Dragon built an entire dragon war. They can't build a Venmo schedule.
Keir Starmer lasted longer in office than the last time they promised to pay you back.
Messi broke a world record today. This debt is also historic. Worse vibes.
They owe you more than VP Vance's Zurich airfare. Start invoicing.
They owe you like Iran owes the West: everything, in theory, on a 60-day clock.
You've become Canada in this dynamic. Winning by six. Getting zero credit.
House of the Dragon has fewer unresolved debts and it's a show about a civil war.
The World Cup prize money could cover this. Start learning what offside means.
Iran signed a nuclear roadmap faster than they'll sign your Venmo request.
Britain got a resignation speech. You got a debtor. Only one came with apologies.
